title: Surface compression failures + god-mode preflight check
current_state: (a) agent/truncation.py:104-105: when both Groq and Haiku compression LLMs fail, compress_messages_with_groq silently returns the original uncompressed messages list. The caller in pi_agent.py:_truncate_messages_safely has no way to know compression failed. The next turn the list is still >= threshold, compression is re-attempted, both LLMs may fail again â†’ burns API quota with no progress. (b) pi_agent.py:539-541: typing 'god mode' switches self.mode='god' without verifying that god_memory.db exists or that GOD_SUPABASE_URL != SUPABASE_URL. If god mode is misconfigured, the switch silently succeeds but the first tool call fails (often with a confusing 'database is locked' or 'duplicate key' error).
structural_problem: Both bugs share a 'fallback that silently returns invalid state' pattern. The caller continues as if everything is fine; the user only finds out when downstream behaviour breaks.
target_state: (a) compress_messages_with_groq raises CompressionFailed(original_messages) when both LLMs fail. pi_agent.py:_truncate_messages_safely catches CompressionFailed and falls back to hard-truncate (keep last N messages, drop the rest) + track_silent. The loop cannot spin: hard truncate guarantees progress. (b) god-mode switch has a preflight check: file existence of GOD_MEMORY_DB, inequality of GOD_SUPABASE_URL vs SUPABASE_URL. Fails loud with a one-line user message; does NOT switch mode.
files_affected: agent/truncation.py (add CompressionFailed exception, raise on both-LLM-fail)
testing/test_compression_fallback.py (UPDATE â€” both-fail case now expects raise + hard truncate)
design_notes: (a) Add class CompressionFailed(Exception) to agent/truncation.py with attribute .original_messages
(a) At line 104-105 area: replace 'return messages' (silent) with 'raise CompressionFailed(messages)'

(a) In pi_agent.py:_truncate_messages_safely, wrap the compress call in try/except CompressionFailed: track_silent('compression.both_llms_failed', e) + hard_truncate (keep last 12 user/assistant pairs from e.original_messages)
(a) Hard truncate must preserve message-pair integrity: trim to even boundary so tool_use/tool_result pairs stay matched (or filter out unmatched tool_use lines)

(b) Preflight check helper: _check_god_mode_available() -> tuple[bool, str] returning (ok, reason). Reasons: 'god_memory.db not found at <path>', 'GOD_SUPABASE_URL not set', 'GOD_SUPABASE_URL == SUPABASE_URL (would leak)', 'agent/god.py module not importable'.
(b) Path checks use Path(...).exists(); URL equality check uses os.getenv with fallback empty
(b) Print one-line failure reason; do NOT raise (user already typed the switch, they need a clear refusal, not a crash)
(b) Existing god-mode tests may need to mock the preflight to return ok=True for non-environment tests
success_criteria: (a) When Groq and Haiku both raise, _truncate_messages_safely keeps last 12 messages and emits a track_silent record
(a) Repeated calls to _truncate_messages_safely with both LLMs broken do NOT call the LLMs again until the message count drops (no infinite retry spin)
(b) typing 'god mode' when god_memory.db does not exist â†’ mode stays unchanged + clear message printed
(b) typing 'god mode' when GOD_SUPABASE_URL == SUPABASE_URL â†’ mode stays unchanged + clear message printed
(b) typing 'god mode' with valid env â†’ mode becomes 'god' (regression unchanged)
risk_notes: (a) Hard truncate to 12 messages may drop tool_use/tool_result orphans â†’ Anthropic API rejects the next call. Mitigation: filter pairs as part of truncation. (b) Existing tests that set self.mode='god' directly are unaffected; only the user-typed switch path is gated.
migration_plan: 1. Add 'class CompressionFailed(Exception)' to agent/truncation.py with __init__ that stores original_messages
2. Replace silent 'return messages' on both-LLM-fail with 'raise CompressionFailed(messages)'
3. In pi_agent.py:_truncate_messages_safely: wrap compress call; on CompressionFailed, track_silent + hard truncate preserving tool_use/tool_result pairs
4. Update testing/test_compression_fallback.py:test_both_llms_fail â€” now expects either (a) CompressionFailed raised, or (b) hard-truncated output from caller wrapper
6. Edit god-mode switch handler (around pi_agent.py:539): call preflight, refuse with message on failure
7. Add testing/test_god_preflight.py â€” 3 cases: missing db, equal URLs, valid env
test_plan: test_compression_both_fail_raises â€” patch Groq AND Haiku to raise; assert CompressionFailed raised with .original_messages set
test_truncate_safely_catches_compression_fail â€” same setup at caller level; assert self.messages truncated, no spin
test_truncate_preserves_tool_pairs â€” input contains orphan tool_use; assert output has matched pairs only
test_god_preflight_missing_db â€” patch Path.exists â†’ False; switch refused; mode unchanged
test_god_preflight_equal_urls â€” set env GOD_SUPABASE_URL == SUPABASE_URL; switch refused
test_god_preflight_valid â€” both checks pass; switch succeeds
